abstract: Software defect prediction (SDP) is an important building block in enhancing software reliability through the identification of faulty modules prior to their deployment. In spite of progress made in applying deep learning to SDP, current models are prone to static learning processes, higher computation costs, and poor scalability for application to intricate software environments. These are the reasons behind elevated error rates, increased processing times, and decreased responsiveness in dynamic defect detection applications. Mitigating these challenges, a new framework, PathOpt-DUN, is introduced, which combines a Dynamic Path-Controllable Deep Unfolding Network (DPDUN) with Planet Optimization (PO) for optimal and precise defect detection. The approach utilizes software defect repositories under NASA's Metrics Data Program (MDP), with primary attributes including Lines of Code (LOC) and Halstead complexity measures. Preprocessing is applied via Global-Local Depth Normalization (GLDN) to provide consistent scaling and equally distributed features. The DPDUN model adaptively modulates learning pathways during training in response to changing patterns of defects, whereas PO optimizes hyperparameters to achieve the best convergence rate and prediction accuracy. Such dynamic coupling successfully balances defect detection accuracy with computational complexity. Experimental results on NASAMDP datasets show that PathOpt-DUN reaches 99.9% accuracy, 0.1% error rate, 99.7% fault detection rate, and lower computational time compared with state-of-the-art baseline models. These findings validate the superiority of PathOpt-DUN in providing robust defect prediction with greater reliability, scalability, and lower computational overhead. By overcoming major limitations of traditional methods, PathOpt-DUN becomes a useful and versatile solution for enhancing software defect detection in large-scale and dynamic software systems.
title_zh: 动态路径可控的深度展开网络结合行星优化：一种可扩展且高效的软件缺陷检测方法
abstract_zh: 软件缺陷预测（SDP）是通过在模块部署前识别出存在缺陷的模块，从而提升软件可靠性的关键环节。尽管深度学习在SDP领域已取得一定进展，但现有模型仍存在静态学习过程、计算成本高以及在复杂软件环境中可扩展性差等问题。这些问题导致动态缺陷检测应用中出现更高的错误率、更长的处理时间以及响应能力下降。为解决上述挑战，本文提出一种新型框架——PathOpt-DUN，该框架结合了动态路径可控深度展开网络（DPDUN）与行星优化（PO）算法，以实现高效且精准的缺陷检测。该方法基于NASA度量数据计划（MDP）中的软件缺陷数据仓库，主要采用代码行数（LOC）和Halstead复杂度度量等核心特征。通过全局-局部深度归一化（GLDN）进行预处理，确保特征的统一缩放与均衡分布。DPDUN模型在训练过程中能够根据缺陷模式的变化自适应地调节学习路径，而PO算法则用于优化超参数，以实现最佳的收敛速度与预测精度。这种动态耦合机制有效平衡了缺陷检测的准确性与计算复杂度。在NASAMDP数据集上的实验结果表明，PathOpt-DUN在各项指标上均优于当前最先进的基线模型：准确率达到99.9%，错误率仅为0.1%，缺陷检测率达99.7%，同时计算时间更短。实验结果验证了PathOpt-DUN在提供稳健缺陷预测方面具有显著优势，具备更高的可靠性、可扩展性以及更低的计算开销。通过克服传统方法的主要局限，PathOpt-DUN成为提升大规模、动态软件系统中缺陷检测能力的一种实用且通用的解决方案。

title: Towards Optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Amar Wissam Houria, Azouaou Faiçal
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Theoretical and Applicative Aspects of Computer Science (ICTAACS)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a transformative paradigm for enhancing Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding responses with external, non-parametric knowledge to enhance outputs and reduce hallucinations; however, the naïve retrieve-then-read approach faces significant limitations, necessitating optimization across the entire pipeline. This comprehensive survey delves into the diverse landscape of these optimization methodologies, moving beyond traditional approaches to explore innovative strategies that address critical challenges. We propose a four-stage taxonomy (Indexing, Retrieval, Augmentation, Generation) to structure the landscape of RAG optimization techniques, providing a detailed examination of chunking strategies, embedding techniques, query expansion methods, context compression algorithms, and prompt engineering approaches. Furthermore, we investigate recent innovations such as adaptive retrieval, hybrid architectures, and joint optimization techniques that synergistically combine improvements across multiple stages for enhanced end-to-end performance. Based on our synthesis of the existing literature, significant challenges remain in the development of effective RAG systems. Specifically, further research is needed to establish robust evaluation metrics, address computational limitations, and adapt these systems for use in diverse domains.
title_zh: 面向优化的检索增强生成：全面综述
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（Retrieval-Augmented Generation, RAG）作为一种变革性范式，通过引入外部非参数化知识来增强大型语言模型（LLMs）的输出，有效提升了生成结果的质量并减少了幻觉现象；然而，传统的“先检索后生成”方法存在诸多局限性，亟需对整个流程进行全面优化。本综述系统性地探讨了RAG优化方法的多样化发展，突破传统范式，深入分析了应对关键挑战的创新策略。我们提出一个四阶段分类体系（索引、检索、增强、生成），用以系统梳理RAG优化技术的演进脉络，详细考察了文本分块策略、嵌入表示技术、查询扩展方法、上下文压缩算法以及提示工程方案。此外，我们还研究了近年来的前沿进展，包括自适应检索、混合架构设计以及联合优化技术，这些方法通过协同提升多个阶段的性能，显著增强了端到端系统的表现。基于对现有文献的综合分析，我们发现构建高效RAG系统仍面临诸多挑战，尤其需要进一步研究建立可靠的评估指标体系，突破计算资源瓶颈，并推动系统在多样化应用场景中的适应性与泛化能力。

title: Automatic File Renaming Using Fine-Tuned BERT and Pattern-Based Entity Extraction
authors: Kenneth Lay, Bryan Sujoso, Andrew Tanuwijaya, Raymond Bahana, Feri Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
abstract: In the current modern digital age, with the exception of a few handful of diligent students, the vast majority of students do not bother naming their files, regardless of what course or class it may be. Whether it be a Python file for a calculator project or an Excel spreadsheet containing some details for their final project, many students still name their files into something that may make sense to them in the moment, but be less recognizable in the end. The aim of this project is to attempt to create a website that contains a system that allows students to upload their files and allow it to rename itself in a more effective and organized manner. This is done through a fine-tuned BERT-based Named Entity Recognition (NER) model, which processes the document's content to identify key entities such as author, title, and unique IDs. Regular Expressions (Regex) were also used to attempt to increase the accuracy and handle specific patterns, to try and ensure the extraction of details from the compatible file formats are accurate. Another discovery made was that the fine-tuned model achieved an accuracy of 86% and a score of 0.87 F1, which outperforms the other pre-trained BERT models. The system is able to identify and extract author names, titles, and IDs from unstructured text to a consistent file name format.
title_zh: 使用微调BERT与基于模式的实体提取实现自动文件重命名
abstract_zh: 在当今现代数字时代，除了极少数勤奋的学生外，绝大多数学生在提交作业时从不给文件命名，无论课程或项目为何。无论是用于计算器项目的Python文件，还是包含最终项目细节的Excel电子表格，许多学生仍会将文件命名为仅在当下对自己有意义的名称，但最终却难以辨认。本项目旨在开发一个网站系统，允许学生上传文件，并自动将其重命名为更有效、更规范的格式。该系统通过一个经过微调的基于BERT的命名实体识别（NER）模型实现，该模型分析文档内容，识别出作者、标题和唯一ID等关键信息。同时，还结合正则表达式（Regex）来进一步提升识别准确率，以处理特定模式，确保从兼容文件格式中准确提取相关信息。另一项重要发现是，该微调模型达到了86%的准确率和0.87的F1分数，优于其他预训练BERT模型。该系统能够从非结构化文本中准确识别并提取作者姓名、标题和ID，并将其统一转换为规范的文件命名格式。

title: AndroByte: LLM-Driven Privacy Analysis through Bytecode Summarization and Dynamic Dataflow Call Graph Generation
authors: Mst Eshita Khatun, Lamine Noureddine, Zhiyong Sui, Aisha Ali-Gombe
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: With the exponential growth in mobile applications, protecting user privacy has become even more crucial. Android applications are often known for collecting, storing, and sharing sensitive user information such as contacts, location, camera, and microphone data, often without the user's clear consent or awareness, raising significant privacy risks and exposure. In the context of privacy assessment, dataflow analysis is particularly valuable for identifying data usage and potential leaks. Traditionally, this type of analysis has relied on formal methods, heuristics, and rule-based matching. However, these techniques are often complex to implement and prone to errors, such as taint explosion for large programs. Moreover, most existing Android dataflow analysis methods depend heavily on predefined list of sinks, limiting their flexibility and scalability. To address the limitations of these existing techniques, we propose AndroByte, an AI-driven privacy analysis tool that leverages the reasoning of a large language model (LLM) on bytecode summarization to dynamically generate accurate and explainable dataflow call graphs from static code analysis. AndroByte achieves a significant Fß-Score of 89% in generating dynamic dataflow call graphs on the fly, outperforming the effectiveness of traditional tools like FlowDroid and Amandroid in leak detection without relying on predefined propagation rules or sink lists. Moreover, AndroByte's iterative bytecode summarization provides comprehensive and explainable insights into dataflow and leak detection, achieving high, quantifiable scores based on the G-Eval metric.
title_zh: AndroByte：通过字节码摘要与动态数据流调用图生成实现的LLM驱动隐私分析
abstract_zh:
随着移动应用的指数级增长，保护用户隐私变得愈发重要。Android应用通常会收集、存储并共享敏感的用户信息，如联系人、位置、摄像头和麦克风数据，且往往在用户未明确同意或知情的情况下进行，这带来了严重的隐私风险和暴露隐患。在隐私评估的背景下，数据流分析对于识别数据使用情况及潜在泄露具有重要价值。传统上，这类分析依赖于形式化方法、启发式规则和基于规则的匹配技术。然而，这些方法通常实现复杂，容易出错，例如在大型程序中容易出现污点传播爆炸问题。此外，大多数现有的Android数据流分析方法严重依赖预定义的“接收端”（sinks）列表，限制了其灵活性和可扩展性。

为克服现有技术的局限性，我们提出了AndroByte——一种基于人工智能的隐私分析工具。该工具利用大型语言模型（LLM）对字节码进行推理与摘要，从静态代码分析中动态生成准确且可解释的数据流调用图。AndroByte在实时生成动态数据流调用图方面取得了89%的Fβ分数，显著优于传统工具（如FlowDroid和Amandroid）在漏洞检测方面的表现，且无需依赖预定义的传播规则或接收端列表。此外，AndroByte通过迭代式字节码摘要，提供了全面且可解释的数据流与漏洞检测洞察，基于G-Eval指标取得了高且可量化的评估分数。

title: In Pursuit of Lean OS Kernels: Improving Configuration-Based Debloating
authors: Akshith Gunasekaran, Gabriel Ritter, Rakesh B. Bobba
publish: 2025 IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC)
abstract: Modern OS kernels are bloated to support diverse hardware and features, yet most deployments use only a small subset. This paper advances configuration-based kernel debloating to reduce attack surface and code size in systems with known workloads. We present Tracie,a trace-based specialization tool that improves precision through better workload attribution and trace-to-config mapping, achieving a 21.67% reduction—outperforming prior work by 8%—and removing 1 additional CVEs. We also introduce Dice, a novel trace-free, dependency-aware debloating approach that iteratively prunes the configuration graph, achieving 35.59% reduction and eliminating 10 more CVEs. Finally, we empirically analyze the Linux kconfig system and show that structural limitations—such as coarse granularity and rigid dependencies—constrain the effectiveness of configuration-based debloating.
title_zh: 追求精简的OS内核：改进基于配置的去冗余技术
abstract_zh: 现代操作系统内核为了支持多样化的硬件和功能而变得臃肿，但大多数部署场景仅使用其中一小部分功能。本文推进了基于配置的内核去冗化技术，旨在针对已知工作负载的系统减少攻击面和代码体积。我们提出了 Tracie，一种基于追踪的专用化工具，通过更精确的工作负载归属和追踪到配置的映射，显著提升了去冗化的精度，实现了21.67%的代码体积缩减，较之前的工作提升了8%，并额外消除了1个CVE漏洞。此外，我们还引入了 Dice，一种全新的无追踪、依赖感知的去冗化方法，通过迭代地修剪配置图，实现了35.59%的缩减率，并额外消除了10个CVE漏洞。最后，我们对 Linux 的 kconfig 系统进行了实证分析，发现其结构性限制——如粒度粗略和依赖关系僵化——严重制约了基于配置的去冗化技术的有效性。

title: A Comprehensive Review of Language Models: Evolution, Applications, and Future Directions
authors: Muhammad Aamir, Ziaur Rahman, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Jameel Ahmed Bhutto, Nomica Choudhry, Waheed Ahmed Abro
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computer, Vision and Intelligent Technology (ICCVIT)
abstract: Language models have fundamentally transformed natural language processing (NLP) and artificial intelligence (AI) over the past decade. This comprehensive review examines their evolution from rule-based systems to transformer-based architectures, highlighting major breakthroughs and diverse applications. We analyze the transition to large-scale pretrained models and the emergence of multimodal and multilingual capabilities. Beyond technological advances, we assess their impact across healthcare, finance, and education. However, significant challenges remain, including computational costs, ethical concerns, and generalization limitations. We identify future research directions focusing on efficiency, interpretability, and bias mitigation. This review serves as a vital resource for researchers and practitioners, providing insights into current advances and future possibilities in language modeling.
title_zh: 语言模型的全面综述：演进、应用与未来方向
abstract_zh: 过去十年中，语言模型从根本上改变了自然语言处理（NLP）和人工智能（AI）领域。本文全面回顾了语言模型从基于规则的系统到基于Transformer架构的演进历程，重点阐述了重大突破及多样化应用。我们分析了大规模预训练模型的兴起，以及多模态和多语言能力的出现。除了技术进步之外，我们还评估了这些模型在医疗、金融和教育等领域的深远影响。然而，仍存在诸多挑战，包括计算成本高昂、伦理问题以及泛化能力的局限性。本文还指出了未来研究方向，重点关注效率提升、可解释性增强以及偏见缓解。本综述为研究人员和实践者提供了宝贵的资源，深入揭示了语言建模领域的最新进展与未来潜力。

title: MTL-CR: A Multitask Learning Approach for Code Representation
authors: Dongxu Yu, Feiqiao Mao, Xingyang Du, Yifan Bai
abstract: Code representation plays a fundamental role in enabling a wide range of code intelligence tasks, such as code translation, bug fixing, and code completion. Existing approaches typically adopt task-specific modeling paradigms, training separate models for individual downstream tasks. However, such methods often fail to capture semantic and structural commonalities across tasks, resulting in limited generalization and transferability. Therefore, we propose MTL-CR (Multitask Learning for Code Representation), a unified multitask learning framework that jointly optimizes multiple code-related tasks to learn more generalizable and robust code representations. To balance optimization dynamics among tasks, we further introduce a dynamic task weighting strategy based on taskspecific learning speeds. Experimental results demonstrate that MTL-CR consistently outperforms single task baselines and static multitask methods in various downstream tasks and evaluation metrics. For example, in the code translation task, it improves Accuracy and BLEU by 6.29% and 2.33%.
title_zh: MTL-CR：一种用于代码表示的多任务学习方法
abstract_zh: 代码表示在实现多种代码智能任务中起着基础性作用，例如代码翻译、缺陷修复和代码补全。现有方法通常采用针对特定任务的建模范式，为每个下游任务单独训练模型。然而，这类方法往往难以捕捉不同任务之间的语义与结构共性，导致泛化能力和迁移能力受限。为此，我们提出了 MTL-CR（多任务学习用于代码表示），这是一个统一的多任务学习框架，通过联合优化多个代码相关任务，以学习更具泛化性和鲁棒性的代码表示。为了平衡各任务间的优化动态，我们进一步引入了一种基于任务特定学习速度的动态任务加权策略。实验结果表明，MTL-CR 在多种下游任务和评估指标上均持续优于单任务基线模型以及静态多任务方法。例如，在代码翻译任务中，其准确率（Accuracy）和 BLEU 分数分别提升了 6.29% 和 2.33%。

title: Recovering Variable Names in the Decompiled Code Based on Multi-Task Learning
authors: Jiayi Li, Jingxuan Zhang, Jian He, Jingjing Gu, Weiqin Zou
abstract: Decompilation is one of the key techniques in software reverse engineering and is widely used in security-related tasks such as malware analysis and vulnerability detection. Software is typically released in binary form with symbol information stripped. Although decompilers are capable of reconstructing a lot of the information lost during compilation, they often fail to recover meaningful variable names. As a result, the readability of the decompiled code is significantly reduced, severely hindering the efficiency of reverse analysis. To address this problem, we propose a novel variable name recovery approach ReDevar for the decompiled code based on Multi-task Learning (MTL), which takes variable name recovery as the main task and introduces two additional tasks, including data source prediction and name complexity prediction, corresponding to two aspects of semantic understanding and semantic transformation of variables in the decompiled code. Data source prediction simulates the data flow relationship among variable instances, assisting ReDevar better understand the semantics of variables. The name complexity prediction task enables ReDevar to perceive the complexity of the expected variable name at a mask position, obtaining appropriate semantic transformation results. They are both jointly trained with the variable name recovery task to improve the understanding of ReDevar for variable semantics and name composition. We conducted a series of experiments to validate the effectiveness of ReDevar. Experimental results show that ReDevar achieves top- 1 accuracy of $54.44 \%$ on the VarCorpus benchmark dataset, 2.81% and 1.50% higher than the state-of-the-art approaches VarBERT and Resym respectively. In addition, we also evaluated the performance of ReDevar under various conditions, including different dataset splitting strategies, different decompilers, and different optimization levels. The results demonstrate that ReDevar generalizes well across all settings. Furthermore, the ablation study indicates that the two auxiliary tasks we introduced in ReDevar are both beneficial for the variable name recovery task.
title_zh: 基于多任务学习的反编译代码中变量名恢复
abstract_zh:
反编译是软件逆向工程中的关键技术之一，广泛应用于恶意软件分析、漏洞检测等安全相关任务中。软件通常以二进制形式发布，且符号信息已被剥离。尽管反编译器能够重建编译过程中丢失的大量信息，但往往无法恢复有意义的变量名，导致反编译代码的可读性显著降低，严重制约了逆向分析的效率。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种基于多任务学习（Multi-task Learning, MTL）的新型变量名恢复方法 ReDevar，将变量名恢复作为主任务，并引入两个辅助任务：数据源预测和名称复杂度预测。这两个辅助任务分别对应于反编译代码中变量语义理解与语义转换的两个方面。数据源预测模拟了变量实例之间的数据流关系，帮助 ReDevar 更好地理解变量的语义；名称复杂度预测任务使 ReDevar 能够感知在掩码位置处期望变量名的复杂程度，从而获得更合适的语义转换结果。主任务与两个辅助任务联合训练，以提升 ReDevar 对变量语义及命名构成的理解能力。

我们开展了一系列实验以验证 ReDevar 的有效性。实验结果表明，在 VarCorpus 基准数据集上，ReDevar 的 top-1 准确率达到 54.44%，分别比当前最先进的方法 VarBERT 和 Resym 提高了 2.81% 和 1.50%。此外，我们还在多种条件下评估了 ReDevar 的性能，包括不同的数据集划分策略、不同的反编译器以及不同的优化级别。结果表明，ReDevar 在所有设置下均表现出良好的泛化能力。进一步的消融实验也证实，我们引入的两个辅助任务均对变量名恢复任务具有积极贡献。

title: MSWT: Multi-Scale Wavelet Transformer for Dynamic Feature-Based Student Program Error Identification
authors: Lingzhong Yu, Qing Zhou, Subo Zhao, Siyuan Wang
abstract: Automatic identification and classification of student programming errors represents a fundamental challenge in intelligent programming education. Student code exhibits unique error patterns that differ significantly from professional code, with logic and algorithmic errors being particularly challenging due to their concealment and diverse manifestations. Traditional static analysis methods struggle with complex semantic errors, while existing deep learning approaches lack multi-scale modeling capabilities for program execution dynamics. To address these limitations, we propose a Multi-Scale Wavelet Transformer (MSWT) model that integrates wavelet transforms with deep learning techniques. The model comprises four core modules: a wavelet transform feature enhancement module for multi-scale time-frequency analysis, a parallel CNN-BiLSTM encoder for capturing local patterns and long-term dependencies, an adaptive weighting module for fusing static and dynamic features, and a multi-head self-attention classifier for final prediction. Experiments on 7,007 real code samples from 198 students demonstrate that MSWT achieves 83.91% weighted F1-score on 10-class error classification, out-performing the best baseline by 5.72 percentage points. This work is the first to introduce wavelet transforms into program error analysis and provides interpretable error localization for programming education.
title_zh: MSWT：用于基于动态特征的学生程序错误识别的多尺度小波变换模型
abstract_zh: 学生编程错误的自动识别与分类是智能编程教育中的一个基本挑战。学生代码呈现出与专业代码显著不同的错误模式，其中逻辑和算法类错误尤为棘手，因其隐蔽性强且表现形式多样。传统的静态分析方法难以应对复杂的语义错误，而现有的深度学习方法在程序执行动态的多尺度建模方面能力不足。为解决上述局限，本文提出一种多尺度小波变换Transformer（MSWT）模型，将小波变换与深度学习技术相结合。该模型包含四个核心模块：用于多尺度时频分析的小波变换特征增强模块、用于捕捉局部模式与长期依赖的并行CNN-BiLSTM编码器、用于融合静态与动态特征的自适应加权模块，以及用于最终预测的多头自注意力分类器。在来自198名学生的7,007个真实代码样本上的实验表明，MSWT在10类错误分类任务中取得了83.91%的加权F1分数，优于最佳基线模型5.72个百分点。本研究首次将小波变换引入程序错误分析领域，并为编程教育提供了可解释的错误定位能力。

title: KG-Code: A Knowledge Graph Guided Pre-Training Approach for Code Representation
authors: Hanyu Zhang, Tomoji Kishi
abstract: Recent years, with the widespread usage of large code pre-trained model, the application of deep learning in Software Engineering (SE) tasks has made significant progress. To achieve better performance, it is important to capture the code features during pre-training process. The existing studies have made many attempts for this challenge, such as trying to introduce Syntax Tree (AST) or data flow graph into pre-training process. However, existing studies still have limitations in the comprehensiveness and simplicity of capturing code features. Knowledge graphs (KGs), which represent entities and their relationships in graph structures, have been extensively applied in intelligent systems for domain-specific tasks. Inspired by this, we attempted a knowledge graph guided code pre-training approach in this study, hoping that the codespecific knowledge graph could help code pre-trained model achieve better code understanding ability. In our approach, we first proposed a new type of code-specific knowledge graph which integrates syntactic, structural, and semantic information from a code fragment. Next, we extract the code feature triplets and code concept triplets from the code knowledge graph as part of input data. We have also designed a specialized pre-training task to enhance the pre-trained model’s code feature comprehension ability. To evaluate our approach, we trained a baseline pre-trained model (KG-Code) and validated its effectiveness on two code understanding downstream tasks: code summarization and code clone detection. The experimental results show that introducing knowledge graph as guidance in the pre-training process could efficiently improve the code understanding ability.
title_zh: KG-Code：一种基于知识图谱的代码表示预训练方法
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着大规模代码预训练模型的广泛应用，深度学习在软件工程（SE）任务中的应用取得了显著进展。为了获得更好的性能，捕捉代码特征在预训练过程中显得尤为重要。现有研究为此做出了诸多尝试，例如尝试将语法树（AST）或数据流图引入预训练过程。然而，现有方法在全面性和简洁性方面仍存在局限，难以充分捕捉代码特征。知识图谱（KG）以图结构表示实体及其关系，在特定领域任务的智能系统中已得到广泛应用。受此启发，本研究提出了一种基于知识图谱引导的代码预训练方法，旨在利用代码特定知识图谱提升预训练模型的代码理解能力。在该方法中，我们首先提出了一种新型的代码特定知识图谱，该图谱整合了代码片段中的语法、结构和语义信息。随后，从代码知识图谱中提取代码特征三元组和代码概念三元组，作为预训练输入数据的一部分。同时，我们设计了一项专门的预训练任务，以增强预训练模型对代码特征的理解能力。为评估所提方法的有效性，我们训练了一个基线预训练模型（KG-Code），并在两个代码理解下游任务——代码摘要生成和代码克隆检测上进行了验证。实验结果表明，在预训练过程中引入知识图谱作为引导，能够有效提升模型的代码理解能力。

title: Trace: Test Repair via Agent-based Context Extraction with LLMs
authors: Jingxiang Tu, Bo Lin, Yihao Qin, Shangwen Wang, Liqian Chen, Xiaoguang Mao
abstract: As software evolves, test code must be co-maintained to ensure quality, but it often becomes obsolete, leading to failures that mislead developers and increase maintenance overhead. While recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches show promise for repairing obsolete tests, their effectiveness is constrained by a critical challenge: providing comprehensive, repository-level context without overwhelming the models’ input limits. Fixed retrieval strategies often fail to capture the diverse dependencies required for complex repairs. In this paper, we present Trace, a retrieve-agent-based repository-level test repair method. The Trace selectively retrieves crucial context by analyzing (1) class-level structures to understand internal changes, (2) caller methods to capture real-world usage patterns, and (3) related files along the call graph to trace transitive dependencies. This multi-faceted context provides the LLM with a precise and concise understanding of the necessary code environment. We evaluated Trace on a dataset of real-world Java test updates, where it demonstrated superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art baselines. Our results confirm that a structured, adaptive retrieval process is key to unlocking the full potential of LLMs for automated test maintenance.
title_zh: 基于代理的上下文提取结合大语言模型的测试修复方法
abstract_zh: 随着软件的演进，测试代码必须同步维护以保证质量，但测试代码常常变得过时，导致错误的失败信息误导开发者，并增加维护成本。尽管近期基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法在修复过时测试方面展现出潜力，但其效果受到一个关键挑战的制约：在不超出模型输入限制的前提下，提供全面的仓库级上下文。固定的检索策略往往无法捕捉复杂修复所需的多样化依赖关系。本文提出Trace，一种基于检索-代理的仓库级测试修复方法。Trace通过分析（1）类级结构以理解内部变更，（2）调用方法以捕捉实际使用模式，以及（3）调用图中的相关文件以追踪传递依赖，从而有选择性地获取关键上下文。这种多维度的上下文为LLM提供了精确且简洁的代码环境理解。我们在真实世界Java测试更新数据集上对Trace进行了评估，结果表明其性能优于现有的最先进基线方法。我们的研究结果证实，结构化且自适应的检索过程是充分发挥LLM在自动化测试维护中潜力的关键。

title: Feature-based Transfer Learning in Cross-Project Defect Prediction: A Systematic Review
authors: Yunpeng Wang, Dongcheng Li, Man Zhao, W. Eric Wong
abstract: This review examines recent feature-based transfer learning techniques for Cross-Project Software Defect Prediction. We summarize representative approaches in five categories-feature selection, feature mapping/alignment, deep/adversarial learning, semantics-enhanced transfer, and hybrid/multi-source designs-and discuss their reported effectiveness across common benchmark datasets. The review highlights trade-offs between predictive accuracy, computational cost, and model interpretability, and concludes with open challenges and directions for future work.
title_zh: 基于特征的跨项目缺陷预测迁移学习：一项系统性综述
abstract_zh: 本综述探讨了近期基于特征的迁移学习技术在跨项目软件缺陷预测中的应用。我们总结了五类代表性方法：特征选择、特征映射/对齐、深度/对抗学习、语义增强迁移以及混合/多源设计，并讨论了这些方法在常用基准数据集上的实际效果。综述指出了预测准确率、计算成本与模型可解释性之间的权衡关系，并最后提出了当前面临的开放性挑战及未来研究方向。
